objective: Adversarial review of the T02 plan for an OpenAI-compatible chat client + health probe in core::explore::client, verified against the actual repo (T01 config, Cargo deps, stack checklist).
key_changes: Verdict: Approved — all 7 task acceptance criteria covered by concrete plan sections; mapping table in PLAN_REVIEW.md.
Verified independently: ExploreConfig (T01) exists with base_url/model/provider; ureq=2 rustls already in core/Cargo.toml (used by fetch.rs); no new dependency needed.
base_url includes /v1, so {base_url}/chat/completions and {base_url}/models paths are correct for both Ollama and llama.cpp.
Testing strategy matches project convention: inline #[cfg(test)] modules, no HTTP mocks, error-before-connect boundary, cargo test --release --locked + clippy -D warnings.
ClientError Connection-vs-Protocol split via ureq::Error Transport/Status correctly serves the D3 shutdown mapping for T03/T04.
Advisory: tool-role Message must carry tool_call_id per OpenAI schema.
Advisory: llama.cpp /v1/models may report file path/alias, not the configured tag — use tolerant model matching to avoid false ModelMissing.
Advisory: set explicit request timeouts; decide whether mid-generation read timeout maps to Connection (D3) or Protocol.
Advisory: include HTTP error-response body in Protocol/Http errors for actionable messages.
Advisory: use a deterministic unreachable target (e.g. 127.0.0.1:1) for the connection-error test to avoid CI flakiness.
findings: Plan is feasible, complete, and architecture-aligned; no revision items.
Five non-blocking advisories recorded for the implementation phase (tool_call_id, model-id matching, timeouts, error bodies, test determinism).
